_meta.description: Question taxonomy for P3 Maths practice bank. Covers all 23 papers: ACS Junior WA1 (13 questions, original), Ai Tong WA1, plus 21 new papers (WA1: Henry Park, Nan Hua, Nanyang, Raffles Girls, Rosyth, Tao Nan; WA2: ACS Junior, Ai Tong, Henry Park, Maha Bodhi, Nan Hua, Nanyang, Northland, Raffles Girls, Rosyth, St Hilda, Tao Nan; WA3: ACS Junior, Ai Tong, Nan Hua, Rosyth).

term_coverage.WA1: Term 1 — Place Value, Money, Number Patterns, Addition/Subtraction, Ordering Numbers
term_coverage.WA2: Term 2 — Multiplication, Division, Fractions, Geometry (Angles, Parallel/Perpendicular Lines), Data (Bar Graphs)
term_coverage.WA3: Term 3 — likely Measurement (Mass, Length, Volume), Time, Area/Perimeter, Graphs (from partial data)

place_value.label: Place Value
place_value.description: Understanding the positional value of each digit (ones, tens, hundreds, thousands); reading, writing and comparing numbers; expanded form; non-standard decompositions.

money.label: Money
money.description: Counting, adding, subtracting and multiplying with dollars and cents; solving word problems involving cost, change, budgeting and comparisons.

number_patterns.label: Number Patterns
number_patterns.description: Identifying and continuing arithmetic sequences with single or alternating rules; completing pattern webs and grids; finding missing terms.

addition_subtraction.label: Addition and Subtraction
addition_subtraction.description: Adding and subtracting whole numbers up to 10 000; vertical algorithms with regrouping; multi-step word problems.

ordering_numbers.label: Comparing and Ordering Numbers
ordering_numbers.description: Comparing 4-digit numbers; arranging in ascending or descending order; identifying numbers satisfying range and parity constraints.

multiplication.label: Multiplication
multiplication.description: Multiplication tables (2–10); equal groups; multiplication as repeated addition; 2-digit and 3-digit × 1-digit; multiplicative comparison word problems.

division.label: Division
division.description: Division as sharing and grouping; 2-digit and 3-digit ÷ 1-digit; quotient and remainder; real-world rounding up; reverse division.

fractions.label: Fractions
fractions.description: Understanding fractions as parts of a whole; equivalent fractions; comparing and ordering fractions; simplification; addition and subtraction of fractions with related denominators.

geometry_angles.label: Angles
geometry_angles.description: Identifying and naming acute, obtuse and right angles; counting angle types in polygons; marking angles in figures.

geometry_lines.label: Parallel and Perpendicular Lines
geometry_lines.description: Identifying parallel and perpendicular lines in diagrams and shapes; drawing parallel and perpendicular lines on square grids.

data_graphs.label: Data Representation (Bar Graphs)
data_graphs.description: Reading single and grouped/double bar graphs; comparing categories; calculating totals and differences from graphs; drawing missing bars; evaluating true/false statements.

time.label: Time
time.description: Reading analogue and digital clocks; converting between hours and minutes; calculating elapsed time.

measurement_mass.label: Mass
measurement_mass.description: Reading weighing scales; solving mass word problems using kg and g.

measurement_volume.label: Volume
measurement_volume.description: Measuring volume by counting equal containers; volume word problems in litres.

odd_even.label: Odd and Even Numbers
odd_even.description: Identifying odd and even numbers; applying parity constraints when forming or selecting numbers.

combinatorics.label: Combinations (Counting)
combinatorics.description: Systematic listing of combinations; counting distinct pairs or groups chosen from a set.

digit_value_mcq.label: Value of a specific digit in a number (MCQ)
digit_value_mcq.template: What is the value of the digit {digit} in {number}?
digit_value_mcq.notes: Special case: if digit=0, correct answer is 0 regardless of position.

expanded_form_mcq.label: Identify the missing term in an expanded-form equation (MCQ)

identify_digit_by_place_mcq.label: Which digit is in the [place] place? (MCQ)
identify_digit_by_place_mcq.template: In {number}, which digit is in the {place_name} place?

non_standard_units_to_numeral_mcq.label: Non-standard decomposition (e.g. 12 hundreds) → numeral (MCQ)
non_standard_units_to_numeral_mcq.template: {a} thousands {b} hundreds {c} tens {d} ones is the same as ___.
non_standard_units_to_numeral_mcq.notes: One or more counts exceed 9 (e.g. 12 hundreds = 1200). Requires regrouping.

words_to_numeral.label: Convert number words to numerals (short answer)

numeral_to_words.label: Convert numeral to number words (short answer)

expanded_form_find_missing_part.label: Find missing value in partial expanded form (short answer)
expanded_form_find_missing_part.notes: Parts given may span non-adjacent places. E.g. 8765 = 8000 + ___ + 5 → answer is 760 (=700+60).

form_number_from_digits_constrained.label: Form smallest/largest number satisfying parity/digit constraints
form_number_from_digits_constrained.template: Use all the digits {digits} to form the {target} 4-digit {parity} number. Each digit can only be used once.

n_more_less_than_short.label: Find number N more/less than a given number
n_more_less_than_short.template: What is {n} more than {number}? / What is {n} less than {number}?

ordering_numbers_short.label: Arrange a set of numbers in decreasing (or increasing) order
ordering_numbers_short.template:
Arrange the following numbers in {direction} order.
{numbers}

ordering_3_numbers_descending_mcq.label: Arrange 3 numbers in decreasing order (MCQ)
ordering_3_numbers_descending_mcq.template: Arrange the following numbers in decreasing order. {n1}, {n2}, {n3}

ordering_mcq_increasing.label: Arrange 3 numbers in increasing order (MCQ)
ordering_mcq_increasing.template: Arrange the following numbers in increasing order. {n1}, {n2}, {n3}

even_number_in_range_identify.label: Identify the even number within a range from a given set
even_number_in_range_identify.template: Which of the following numbers is an even number that is greater than {lo} but smaller than {hi}? {options}

number_pattern_missing_mcq.label: Find two consecutive missing terms in an arithmetic sequence (MCQ)
number_pattern_missing_mcq.template:
What are the missing numbers in the number pattern?
{a}, {a+d}, {a+2d}, {a+3d}, _____, _____, {a+6d}

number_pattern_alternating_rule_mcq.label: Find next term in sequence with two alternating rules (MCQ)

number_pattern_missing_middle_term.label: Find a missing middle term in an arithmetic sequence (short answer)
number_pattern_missing_middle_term.template: Find the missing number in the number pattern. {a}, ___, {a+2d}, {a+3d}, …

decreasing_pattern_find_missing.label: Find missing term in a decreasing arithmetic sequence

number_pattern_web.label: Number web: central number divided by surrounding divisors
number_pattern_web.template: Look at the number pattern below. What is the missing number? [diagram: centre={n}, surrounding: n÷a, n÷b, n÷c, n÷?]

direct_addition_short.label: Find the sum of two numbers (short answer)

subtraction_difference_mcq.label: Find the difference between two numbers (MCQ)

addition_4digit_mcq.label: Find the sum of two 4-digit numbers (MCQ)

vertical_subtraction_4digit.label: Vertical subtraction of two 4-digit numbers (short answer)

missing_digit_vertical_subtraction.label: Find a hidden digit in a vertical subtraction sum

missing_digit_in_subtraction_equation.label: Find missing digit in equation of form A□BC − D = E
missing_digit_in_subtraction_equation.notes: One digit of the top number is in a box. Recover by: masked_number = difference + subtrahend.

two_number_sum_difference_mcq.label: Given sum and one number, find the difference (MCQ)
two_number_sum_difference_mcq.template: The sum of two numbers is {total}. One of the numbers is {a}. What is the difference between the 2 numbers?

two_number_difference_find_sum.label: Given difference and smaller number, find the sum
two_number_difference_find_sum.template: The difference between two numbers is {diff}. The smaller number is {smaller}. Find the sum of the two numbers.

part_whole_find_whole_word_problem.label: Given remainder after action, find original total
part_whole_find_whole_word_problem.template: After {action} {amount}, {name} had {remainder} left. How many did {name} have at first?

fewer_than_then_total_word_problem.label: B has fewer than A; find total of A and B
fewer_than_then_total_word_problem.template: {a_count} {people/things} [action A]. {fewer} fewer [same] [action B]. How many altogether?

chain_add_subtract_word_problem.label: 3-entity chain: A more/fewer than B, B more/fewer than C; find C
chain_add_subtract_word_problem.template: [A sold/has X]. [B sold Y more/fewer than A]. [C sold Z more/fewer than B]. How many did C [sell/have]?

two_part_comparison_and_total_word_probl.label: Two-part: (a) how many more, (b) total
two_part_comparison_and_total_word_probl.template: There are {a} [A] and {b} [B]. (a) How many more [A] than [B]? (b) How many altogether?

money_multi_item_mcq.label: Total cost of two groups of items with different unit prices (MCQ)
money_multi_item_mcq.template: What is the total cost of {n1} {item1} and {n2} {item2}?

money_change_from_purchase.label: Calculate change after buying multiple items
money_change_from_purchase.template: {name} bought {item1} and {item2}. She gave the cashier ${paid}. How much change?

money_budget_open_ended.label: Find valid item pairs within a budget (open-ended)
money_budget_open_ended.template: {name} had ${budget}. She bought two different {item_type}s. Which two could she buy? How much did she spend?

money_comparison_total.label: Item B costs $X less than item A; find total of both
money_comparison_total.template: {name} bought {item1} for ${price1}. {item2} costs ${less} less. How much altogether?

money_sale_savings.label: Calculate total savings when buying at sale price
money_sale_savings.template: A {item} usually costs ${unit_price}. {name} bought {n} for ${sale_price}. How much did she save?

money_before_after_mcq.label: Given amount after receiving/spending, find original amount (MCQ)
money_before_after_mcq.template: After {name} {received/spent} ${amount}, she had ${final}. How much did she have at first?
money_before_after_mcq.correct_answer_formula: final ∓ amount (subtract if received, add if spent)

two_purchases_find_original_amount.label: Two items bought, money left; find original amount
two_purchases_find_original_amount.template: {name} bought {item1} (${p1}) and {item2} (${p2}). Had ${left} left. How much at first?

multi_expense_change_calculation.label: Multiple expenses, change from given amount
multi_expense_change_calculation.template: {name} spent ${a} on {item1} and ${b} on {item2}. Paid ${note}. How much change?

money_combination_selection_mcq.label: Which combination of 3 items totals exactly $X? (MCQ)
money_combination_selection_mcq.template: Kyan spent ${total} on 3 different items. What did Kyan buy? [options list]
money_combination_selection_mcq.notes: Student must find the combination of 3 items that sums to the target.

count_mixed_notes_and_coins.label: Count total from a mix of notes and coins (image-dependent)
count_mixed_notes_and_coins.template: Count and write the total amount of money. [image of notes and coins]
count_mixed_notes_and_coins.notes: IMAGE ESSENTIAL. Tests ability to identify denominations and sum correctly.

compare_prices_identify_most_expensive.label: Identify most expensive item from price tags (image-dependent)
compare_prices_identify_most_expensive.template: Which item is the most expensive? [image of items with price tags]

difference_money_word_problem.label: How much more did X spend than Y?
difference_money_word_problem.template: {name} spent ${a} on {item1} and ${b} on {item2}. How much more on {item1} than {item2}?

unit_rate_multiply_cost.label: N items cost $X; find cost of M items
unit_rate_multiply_cost.template: A bag of {n} {items} cost ${cost}. How much did {name} pay for {m} {items}?
common_errors: multiplies m × cost ignoring group size

total_then_divide_money.label: Find unit price when N items cost same as known total
total_then_divide_money.template: {name} had enough money to buy {n1} {item1} at ${p1} each. He can also buy {n2} {item2} for same amount. Find cost of each {item2}.

buy_n_get_k_free_promotion.label: Buy-N-get-K-free promotional pricing
buy_n_get_k_free_promotion.template: For every {n} {items} bought, get {k} free at ${c} per {n}. (a) With ${budget}, how many total? (b) To get {target} items, how much?
buy_n_get_k_free_promotion.notes: Each 'deal unit' = n paid + k free = (n+k) items for price c.

money_coin_combination_possible.label: Given specific coins, which amount is achievable? (MCQ)
money_coin_combination_possible.template: The purse contains {coins}. Which of the following amounts could she have donated?
money_coin_combination_possible.notes: IMAGE ESSENTIAL. Student must check which option can be formed from given coin set.

multi_purchase_find_original_amount_word.label: Multi-purchase + money left = find original amount (word problem)
multi_purchase_find_original_amount_word.template: {name} bought {item1} for ${p1} and {item2} for ${p2}. Had ${left} left. How much at first?

multiplication_as_repeated_addition_mcq.label: Express multiplication as repeated addition (MCQ)
multiplication_as_repeated_addition_mcq.notes: Convention: a × b = b added a times (or variant; check which school uses which).

equal_groups_representation_mcq.label: Choose the multiplication that matches a picture of equal groups (MCQ)
equal_groups_representation_mcq.template: Which of the following represents all the {items} in the picture? [image of {groups} groups of {per_group}]
equal_groups_representation_mcq.correct_answer_formula: per_group × groups (or groups × per_group depending on convention)

multiplication_3digit_by_1digit.label: 3-digit × 1-digit multiplication (short answer)
common_errors: place value error in intermediate product

times_as_many_then_total_word_problem.label: A has k times as many as B; find A+B
times_as_many_then_total_word_problem.template: There are {b} {item_B}. The number of {item_A} is {k} times the number of {item_B}. How many {item} altogether?
common_errors: reports A only (forgets to add b)

multiplicative_chain_word_problem.label: Chain: A = k1 × B, B = k2 × C; find A
multiplicative_chain_word_problem.template: {C_name} made {c} {items}. {B_name} made {k1} times as many as {C_name}. {A_name} made {k2} times as many as {B_name}. How many did {A_name} make?

units_method_comparison_word_problem.label: A = k × B, A − B = difference; find A and B using units method
units_method_comparison_word_problem.template: {A_name} scored {k} times as many points as {B_name}. {A_name} scored {diff} more points than {B_name}. How many did they score altogether?

divide_then_multiply_money_word_problem.label: Divide to find number of groups, then multiply for total value

division_missing_divisor_mcq.label: Find missing divisor in A ÷ ___ = B (MCQ)
distractors: nearby integers with no clear relationship

equal_groups_division_word_problem.label: Sharing equally into groups (short answer)
equal_groups_division_word_problem.template: {name} packed {total} {items} into bags. There were {n} {items} in each bag. How many bags?

division_with_remainder_round_up_word_pr.label: Division with remainder requiring ceiling (round up) word problem
division_with_remainder_round_up_word_pr.template: {name} has {total} {items}. She packs {n} per {container}. How many {containers} needed to pack ALL?
division_with_remainder_round_up_word_pr.notes: Key insight: remainder > 0 means one extra container is needed.

quotient_only_short_answer.label: Find the quotient only (short answer)
quotient_only_short_answer.template: What is the quotient when {a} is divided by {b}?

quotient_and_remainder_short_answer.label: Find both quotient and remainder (short answer)
quotient_and_remainder_short_answer.template: Find the quotient and the remainder when {a} is divided by {b}.
quotient_and_remainder_short_answer.correct_answer_formula: quotient = a // b, remainder = a % b

reverse_division_find_dividend.label: Given divisor, quotient and remainder, find the dividend
reverse_division_find_dividend.template: When a number is divided by {d}, the quotient is {q} and the remainder is {r}. What is the number?

measurement_division_word_problem.label: Divide a measured quantity into equal pieces
measurement_division_word_problem.template: {name} has a {item} that is {total} cm long. Cut equally into {n} cm pieces. How many pieces?

equivalent_fractions_missing_numerator_m.label: Find missing numerator in equivalent fraction (MCQ)

fraction_of_figure_simplest_form.label: What fraction of a figure is shaded? Express in simplest form.
fraction_of_figure_simplest_form.template: The figure is made up of {n} identical {shapes}. What fraction is shaded? Express in simplest form.

ordering_fractions_ascending.label: Arrange fractions smallest to largest
ordering_fractions_ascending.template: Arrange the fractions in order. Begin with the smallest. {fractions}
ordering_fractions_ascending.notes: Requires finding LCD or benchmark fractions.

ordering_unit_fractions_descending.label: Arrange unit fractions greatest to smallest
ordering_unit_fractions_descending.template: Arrange the following fractions in order. Begin with the greatest. {unit_fractions}
ordering_unit_fractions_descending.notes: Key insight: for unit fractions, larger denominator = smaller fraction.
common_errors: reverses order (treats larger denominator as larger fraction)

ordering_fractions_greatest_to_smallest_.label: Arrange fractions greatest to smallest (MCQ)
ordering_fractions_greatest_to_smallest_.template: Arrange these fractions from the greatest to the smallest. {fractions}

fraction_simplification.label: Write a fraction in its simplest form (short answer)

fraction_addition_unlike_denominators.label: Add two fractions with different denominators; simplify
fraction_addition_unlike_denominators.template: Find the sum of {a}/{b} and {c}/{d}. Leave your answer in simplest form.
fraction_addition_unlike_denominators.notes: Denominators are typically related (one is a multiple of the other).

fraction_subtraction_find_remainder.label: Two fractions consumed; find fraction remaining
fraction_subtraction_find_remainder.template: {A} ate {a}/{b} of a {item} and {B} ate {c}/{d}. What fraction was left?

missing_fraction_in_equation.label: Find missing fraction in equation [?] + a/b = c/d
missing_fraction_in_equation.template: What is the missing fraction in the box? [box] + {a}/{b} = {c}/{d}

fraction_find_unknown_from_subtraction.label: Spent fraction on A, unknown on B, fraction remaining; find B's fraction
fraction_find_unknown_from_subtraction.template: {name} spent {a}/{b} on {item1} and some on {item2}. Had {c}/{d} left. What fraction on {item2}?

identify_shaded_fraction_from_diagram_mc.label: Which diagram shows a given fraction shaded? (MCQ)
identify_shaded_fraction_from_diagram_mc.template: Which of the following shows that {a}/{b} of the figure is shaded?
identify_shaded_fraction_from_diagram_mc.notes: IMAGE ESSENTIAL. Distractors use figures where parts are unequal, or different fraction is shaded.

fraction_shade_more_to_reach_target.label: Figure has some shaded; how many more to reach target fraction? (MCQ)
fraction_shade_more_to_reach_target.template: A figure of {n} equal squares has {k} already shaded. To have {a}/{b} shaded, how many more?

fraction_eaten_by_two_find_remainder.label: Two people eat fractions; find remaining (short answer)
fraction_eaten_by_two_find_remainder.template: {A} ate {a}/{b} of a pizza and {B} ate {c}/{d}. What fraction was left?

identify_angle_type_mcq.label: Which of the following is an acute/obtuse/right angle? (MCQ)
identify_angle_type_mcq.template: Which of the following is an {angle_type} angle? [four angle diagrams]

identify_obtuse_angle_in_figure_mcq.label: In a figure with labelled angles, which is obtuse? (MCQ)
identify_obtuse_angle_in_figure_mcq.template: In the figure, which angle is an obtuse angle? (1) ∠a (2) ∠b (3) ∠c (4) ∠d

count_angle_type_in_figure_short.label: Count how many angles of a given type are in a figure (short answer)
count_angle_type_in_figure_short.template: In the figure shown below, how many of the marked angles are {angle_type} angles?

mark_angles_in_polygon.label: Mark all angles in a polygon, then count right angles (two-part)
mark_angles_in_polygon.template: (a) Mark the remaining angles inside the shape. (b) How many right angles?
mark_angles_in_polygon.notes: IMAGE ESSENTIAL — polygon with one angle already marked.

identify_parallel_lines_mcq.label: Identify pair of parallel lines from a diagram (MCQ)
identify_parallel_lines_mcq.template: Identify the pair of parallel lines in the figure below.
identify_parallel_lines_mcq.notes: IMAGE ESSENTIAL — grid with labelled lines.

identify_perpendicular_lines_mcq.label: Which line is perpendicular to a given line? (MCQ)
identify_perpendicular_lines_mcq.template: Which line is perpendicular to line {line}?

identify_perpendicular_in_letters.label: Which letter shape has perpendicular lines? (short answer)
identify_perpendicular_in_letters.template: Which of the letters has a pair of perpendicular lines? [A, L, K, O, Z]

name_parallel_lines_from_diagram.label: Name a pair of parallel lines from a labelled diagram (short answer)
name_parallel_lines_from_diagram.template: Study the diagram below. Name a pair of parallel lines.

draw_perpendicular_on_grid.label: Draw a perpendicular line through a point on a grid
draw_perpendicular_on_grid.template: In the grid below, draw a line perpendicular to {line}, passing through point {P}.

draw_parallel_on_grid.label: Draw a parallel line through a point on a grid
draw_parallel_on_grid.template: Draw a line parallel to line {line} that passes through point {P}.

draw_perpendicular_and_parallel_on_grid.label: Draw both a perpendicular and parallel line using given dots on grid (two-part)
draw_perpendicular_and_parallel_on_grid.template: (a) Draw a line perpendicular to {EF} by joining two dots. (b) Draw a line parallel to {EF} by joining two dots. Use arrowheads to show parallel.

identify_perpendicular_parallel_true_sta.label: Given diagram, identify the true statement about line relationships (MCQ)
identify_perpendicular_parallel_true_sta.template: The diagram shows lines on a square grid. Which of the following is true? (1)…(4)…
identify_perpendicular_parallel_true_sta.notes: IMAGE ESSENTIAL. Tests ability to correctly identify ⊥ and // from a labelled grid.

bar_graph_read_single_value_mcq.label: Read one bar value from a bar graph (MCQ)
bar_graph_read_single_value_mcq.template: The bar graph shows {data}. How many {item_type} were {action}?

bar_graph_find_difference.label: How many more/fewer of A than B from bar graph?

bar_graph_count_above_threshold.label: Count bars/values above a given threshold
bar_graph_count_above_threshold.notes: IMAGE ESSENTIAL. Common error: includes threshold value (uses ≥ instead of >).

bar_graph_total_two_categories.label: Find total of two named categories from bar graph
bar_graph_total_two_categories.template: How many {A} and {B} did {subject} sell/collect in total?

bar_graph_complete_given_relationship.label: Calculate and draw a missing bar from given relationship
bar_graph_complete_given_relationship.template: {Day X} had {k} times as many {items} as {Day Y}. Draw the bar for {Day X}.
bar_graph_complete_given_relationship.notes: IMAGE ESSENTIAL — student must draw on the graph.

data_read_then_calculate.label: Read graph value, then apply arithmetic (e.g. multiply by price)
data_read_then_calculate.template: {subject} sold each {item} for ${price}. How much collected from selling all {A} and {B}?

grouped_bar_graph_read_and_compare.label: Read and compare two groups in a double/grouped bar graph
grouped_bar_graph_read_and_compare.template: (a) Which {category} has more {GroupA} than {GroupB}? (b) How many {GroupB} in {category}?

bar_graph_true_false_statements.label: Evaluate true/false statements about a bar graph
bar_graph_true_false_statements.template: Each statement below is either true or false. Write 'T' for true and 'F' for false. (i)… (ii)… (iii)… (iv)…
bar_graph_true_false_statements.notes: IMAGE ESSENTIAL. Tests reading accuracy and comparison skills simultaneously.

bar_graph_equalise_two_groups.label: Transfer items from one group to another to equalise; find amount transferred
bar_graph_equalise_two_groups.template: How many {items} must be taken from {GroupA} and placed in {GroupB} so that both boxes will have the same number?

bar_graph_identify_double.label: Identify which category is double another from bar graph
bar_graph_identify_double.template: Who collected twice as many {items} as {subject}?

bar_graph_find_difference_to_match.label: How many more must one collect to match another (from bar graph)?
bar_graph_find_difference_to_match.template: How many more {items} must {A} collect so that {A} has the same number as {B}?

read_analogue_clock.label: Read time from analogue clock face (short answer)
read_analogue_clock.template: The clock below shows the time when {name} {action}. What time was it?

convert_hours_minutes_to_minutes.label: Convert hours and minutes to total minutes (short answer)
convert_hours_minutes_to_minutes.template: How many minutes are there in {h}h {m} min?

hours_to_minutes_conversion_mcq.label: Convert hours and minutes to total minutes (MCQ)

elapsed_time_word_problem.label: Calculate elapsed time between two given times
elapsed_time_word_problem.template: {name} {action} from {start_time} to {end_time}. How long was it in hours and minutes?

scale_reading_double_mass.label: Read mass from scale, find total for multiple items
scale_reading_double_mass.template: The diagram shows the mass of 1 jar. What is the mass of {n} such jars?

volume_counting_equal_containers_mcq.label: Find total volume by counting equal containers (MCQ)
volume_counting_equal_containers_mcq.template: {name} pours all water from a pail into bottles of {capacity}. What is the total volume?
volume_counting_equal_containers_mcq.notes: IMAGE ESSENTIAL — bottles and pail shown.

combinations_counting_word_problem.label: Count distinct combinations (choosing r from n) by listing
combinations_counting_word_problem.template: {name} has {n} different {items}: {list}. She chooses {r}. How many different ways?
combinations_counting_word_problem.notes: P3 level: solved by systematic listing, not formula.

calendar_reasoning_odd_even.label: Identify a date from clues (odd/even, day-of-week, digit sum)
calendar_reasoning_odd_even.template: The date of the party satisfies: (1) odd number, (2) on a weekend, (3) digit sum = {s}. [calendar shown]

pictorial_algebra_system_of_equations.label: Solve system of equations represented by picture symbols
pictorial_algebra_system_of_equations.notes: IMAGE ESSENTIAL — variables represented as animal/shape pictures.

age_problem_sum_of_ages.label: Find one age when sum of two ages and age difference are given
age_problem_sum_of_ages.template: {A} is {d} years {older/younger} than {B}. How old will {A} be when the sum of their ages is {s}?
age_problem_sum_of_ages.notes: Solved via units method or simultaneous equations at P3 level using guess-and-check or bar model.

reverse_multi_step_word_problem.label: Work backwards from end state through multiple changes to find start
reverse_multi_step_word_problem.template: Started with [?]. {action1} {amount1} [items]. Then {action2} {amount2} [items]. Had {final} at end. Find start.

2x2_place_digit_grid.label: 2×2 Place-Digit Grid
2x2_place_digit_grid.description: Options formed by crossing two place levels (current, one-up) with two perspectives (digit×place, place alone).

shifted_and_stripped_grid.label: Shifted & Stripped Grid
shifted_and_stripped_grid.description: Options slide the digit one place up or down, or strip the place value entirely.

wrong_common_difference.label: Wrong Common Difference
wrong_common_difference.description: Distractors use a systematically smaller common difference (actual d minus 10, 20, 30).

money_quantity_confusion.label: Money vs. Quantity Confusion
money_quantity_confusion.description: Distractors arise from confusing quantity (number of items) with price, or concatenating rather than multiplying.

wrong_operation_variants.label: Wrong Operation Variants
wrong_operation_variants.description: Distractors use wrong operations (add instead of subtract, stop at intermediate result, compound errors).

division_operation_confusion.label: Division Operation Confusion
division_operation_confusion.description: Distractors use the wrong operation: multiply instead of divide, add instead of multiply, etc.

digit_only.label: Bare digit with no place value

adds_quantities_wrong_unit.label: Adds item quantities, applies arbitrary unit

quantity_as_dollars.label: Reads item count as dollar amount

concatenates_quantities_and_denomination.label: Concatenates sum of quantities and sum of prices

incomplete_multistep_other_number.label: Answers intermediate result instead of final answer

wrong_operation_sum_plus_a.label: Adds known number to total instead of subtracting

reversal_ascending_descending.label: Gives ascending order when descending is asked (or vice versa)
reversal_ascending_descending.formula: sorted(numbers, reverse=False) when should be True

floor_division_no_roundup.label: Gives floor quotient without rounding up for remainder

multiplies_instead_of_divides.label: Multiplies when should divide (or vice versa)
multiplies_instead_of_divides.formula: a × b instead of a ÷ b

unit_fraction_order_reversal.label: Orders unit fractions as if larger denominator = larger fraction

bar_graph_off_by_one_gridline.label: Misreads bar length by one gridline interval

place_confusion_off_by_one_up.label: Place mis-identification (one level too high)
place_confusion_off_by_one_up.description: Student identifies the digit correctly but assigns it to the next higher place.
remediation_hints: Write the number in a place-value chart before answering.
Cover all digits except the target; identify its column header.

place_value_conflation.label: Confusing place value with digit value
place_value_conflation.description: Student identifies the correct place but reports the place value (e.g. 10) rather than digit × place (e.g. 70).
remediation_hints: Reinforce: value of digit = digit × place value.
Use base-10 blocks: 7 rods of 10 = 70, not just 1 rod = 10.

place_value_conflation_shifted.label: Wrong place AND reports bare place value
place_value_conflation_shifted.description: Picks the wrong (higher) place and also forgets to multiply by the digit.
remediation_hints: Use a chart; have student circle both the digit and its column.
Explicit two-step: (1) which column? (2) digit × column value.

no_place_value_applied.label: Reports bare digit without applying place value
no_place_value_applied.description: Student reports the digit itself (e.g. 2) rather than its value in context (e.g. 200).
remediation_hints: Compare: the digit 2 in 5207 vs. the number 2 on its own — why different?
Multiply the digit by its column header to get the value.

place_confusion_off_by_one_down.label: Place mis-identification (one level too low)
place_confusion_off_by_one_down.description: Student assigns the digit to the next lower place.
remediation_hints: Practise reading digits from right: ones, tens, hundreds, thousands.
Count place columns from right to left on a chart.

quantity_unit_confusion.label: Confuses item quantity with monetary value
quantity_unit_confusion.description: Adds item counts and treats the result as a price.
remediation_hints: Label every number: '8 stamps', not just '8'.
First find each subtotal: group × price, then add subtotals.

quantity_as_currency.label: Reads item count as a dollar amount
quantity_as_currency.description: Mistakes number of items (e.g. 8) for a dollar amount ($8).
remediation_hints: Write units on every value: '8 stamps × 50¢ = 400¢'.
The number 8 is a count, not money.

numerical_concatenation.label: Concatenates separate totals into a single price
numerical_concatenation.description: Adds quantities (8+7=15) and denominations (20+50=70) separately and reads as $15.70.
remediation_hints: Explicit table: quantity × unit price = subtotal; sum subtotals.
Show the two separate products before adding.

misidentified_common_difference.label: Incorrectly identifies the common difference
misidentified_common_difference.description: Uses a step size that doesn't fit all consecutive pairs.
remediation_hints: Check the difference for two or more consecutive pairs, not just one.
Write the difference above each arrow: 3448 →(40)→ 3488 →(40)→…

incomplete_multistep.label: Stops at an intermediate step
incomplete_multistep.description: Correctly finds an intermediate result but reports it as the final answer.
remediation_hints: Underline exactly what the question asks for before calculating.
Practise identifying 'what is the final question?' in multi-step problems.

wrong_inverse_operation.label: Uses wrong inverse operation to recover an unknown
wrong_inverse_operation.description: Adds the known number to the total instead of subtracting.
remediation_hints: Bar model: whole bar = sum, one part = a, other part = total − a.
If a + b = total, then b = total − a (not total + a).

compound_operation_errors.label: Chains multiple wrong operations
compound_operation_errors.description: Applies the wrong operation at two consecutive steps, compounding the error.
remediation_hints: Break into labelled steps; check each step before proceeding.
Ask: 'Does this intermediate result make sense?'

ordering_direction_confusion.label: Reverses ordering direction (ascending vs descending)
ordering_direction_confusion.description: Arranges numbers in ascending order when descending is asked, or vice versa.
remediation_hints: Highlight the direction word ('begin with smallest' vs 'begin with greatest').
Draw a number line and circle the correct start/end.

division_no_rounding_up.label: Gives floor quotient without rounding up when remainder exists
division_no_rounding_up.description: For real-world problems requiring 'enough containers', student gives exact quotient ignoring the remainder.
remediation_hints: Ask: 'Are all items packed?' If remainder > 0, one more container is needed.
Practise 'ceiling division': 65 ÷ 9 = 7 r 2 → need 8 boxes.

wrong_inverse_operation_mult_div.label: Multiplies when should divide (or vice versa)
wrong_inverse_operation_mult_div.description: Confuses which operation is needed when given a total and rate.
remediation_hints: Draw a bar model: total bar ÷ number of groups = size of each group.
Check: if answer is larger than input, did you multiply? Should you have divided?

unit_fraction_inverse_relationship_ignor.label: Treats larger denominator as larger unit fraction
unit_fraction_inverse_relationship_ignor.description: Student orders 1/10 > 1/3 because 10 > 3, ignoring that unit fractions decrease as denominator increases.
remediation_hints: Use physical fraction strips: 1/10 strip is visibly shorter than 1/3 strip.
Explain: dividing a pizza into 10 slices gives smaller slices than into 3.

graph_reading_imprecision.label: Misreads bar chart value by one or more gridline intervals
graph_reading_imprecision.description: Student reads the wrong gridline when the bar ends between gridlines.
remediation_hints: Always trace a horizontal line from the bar tip to the y-axis.
Identify the scale interval first before reading any bars.
